title: Define queue canon for dispatch ordering
goal: Make registry/queue.json the canonical dispatch queue while keeping task.priority as advisory metadata only, with tests preventing unqueued priority tasks from being claimed.

metadata.source_review: Follow-up from Bureau improvement review: queue canon must not conflict with task.priority.
metadata.safety_boundary: Queue canon affects dispatch eligibility and status reporting only; it does not grant task creation, verification or merge authority.
metadata.outcome: Implemented in Bureau PR #234: registry/queue.json is the dispatch canon; task.priority remains advisory/display metadata; unqueued tasks are not claimable and sort after queued tasks.
validation: Bureau PR #234 GitHub CI passed validate (3.10) and validate (3.12).
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json doctor -> healthy true.
Local before merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . --json registry-truth -> healthy true, 0 findings.
Local before merge: python3 -m pytest tests/test_v2.py tests/test_status_projection.py tests/test_registry_queue.py -q -> passed.
Local before merge: python3 -m pytest -> 359 passed.
Local before merge: python3 -m ruff check . -> passed.
Local before merge: git diff --check -> passed.
Post-merge: python3 -m bureau.cli --root . check -> valid True.

verified_acceptance.queue-canonical-dispatch: tests/test_v2.py covers an unqueued ready task with priority now and asserts dispatcher refuses to claim it.
verified_acceptance.queue-order-before-priority: tests/test_v2.py covers queued tasks sorting before unqueued higher-priority tasks.
verified_acceptance.projection-names-advisory-priority: tests/test_status_projection.py covers task-priority-not-queued with queue_canonical true.
verified_acceptance.docs-state-canon: docs/concurrency.md states registry/queue.json is dispatch canon and task.priority is advisory/display metadata.

acceptance.assertion: Dispatcher refuses to claim ready tasks that are absent from registry/queue.json even when task.priority says now.
Queued tasks sort before unqueued tasks even when an unqueued task has a higher advisory priority rank.
Status projection reports unqueued priority as advisory and names registry/queue.json as the queue canon.
Documentation states that registry/queue.json is canonical for dispatch and task.priority is advisory/display metadata.
